rationale: Registry of canonical path strings (filesystem paths, package paths, executable arguments). When a path is renamed/moved, add the deprecated form to this registry so path-canonical-ripple.ts flags every surviving occurrence in the configured scope. Keep entries focused on string forms that machines verify; semantic recasts (e.g. Tier-8-vs-Tier-1 framing changes) belong in the failure-mode catalog audit layer instead.

paths.note: Plan-023 Tier 1 partial substrate carve-out collapsed shell/renderer into a single apps/desktop workspace. Deprecated forms are written without the trailing slash on purpose — `git grep -F` is a substring match, so `apps/desktop/shell` catches BOTH the literal-path form (`apps/desktop/shell/foo.ts`) AND the executable / CLI form (`pnpm rebuild --filter=apps/desktop/shell better-sqlite3`); the latter is the form that escaped the original PR #24 review per failure-mode-catalog.md row CAT-01. docs/archive/** retains historical references; docs/operations/failure-mode-catalog.md documents the failure pattern itself and must contain the literal deprecated string.
